name: Kyren Williams
headline: Touchdown-rich workhorse worth a Round 3 RB2 bet
body:
Williams keeps stacking RB1 seasons on the strength of usage and scoring, not breakaway speed. Across 17 games in 2025 he turned 259 carries into 1,252 yards at 4.83 a pop, added 36 grabs, and finished RB8 with 227.3 fantasy points. The real engine is the goal line: 63 red-zone opportunities, 18 goal-line carries, and 10 rushing scores plus three through the air. His EPA per play flipped positive to 0.044 after sitting at -0.074 in 2024, and his explosive-run rate climbed to 10 percent even as his snap share dipped to 68 percent.

The ceiling cap is Blake Corum, who now splits early-down work and pushes this toward a committee. Even so, Williams retains the lead role and the high-value touches that make backs fantasy-relevant. With an ADP near RB16 and a Round 3 price, he profiles as a steady low-end RB1, and pairing him with Corum protects the investment.
